scenario: User requests trace of a complete feature through frontend and backend

input.description: User clicks 'Checkout', fills payment form, submits order, receives confirmation email

expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) ask which entry point to trace, (2) scan for candidates (frontend button handler, backend POST /api/orders), (3) trace full call chain from UI through API to database, (4) mark external boundaries (Stripe, SendGrid), (5) confidence-check for gaps, (6) generate Mermaid sequence diagram, (7) write docs/traces/YYYY-MM-DD-order-checkout.md
notes: Should identify entry point as confirmed (source found), not inferred. Should mark external services as [external: name]

scenario: Trace reveals a broken or missing link in the call chain

input.known_path: Login form → POST /api/auth → ? → JWT token returned
input.gap: Middleware that validates credentials is not located in workspace

expected_behavior: During trace, skill finds A → [?] → B gap. Confidence check triggers: C2 (broken link). Skill prepends ⚠️ LOW CONFIDENCE block. Asks user: 'Proceed with partial output or investigate further?'
notes: Test that skill properly detects and reports gaps. Does not guess or skip silently.

scenario: Trace of a feature implemented across multiple files with side effects
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) trace from entry point through service layer to DB mutations, (2) identify all side effects (email sent, verification token created), (3) map boundaries clearly (where data enters/exits each layer), (4) note external service call to SendGrid, (5) write complete trace with Confirmed Facts section listing all touched tables
notes: Test mapping of data boundaries and side effects across component boundaries
